description: This is a scanning electron microscopy (SEM) image showing the surface morphology of oxide protrusions formed on Fe-15wt%Mn-8wt%Al alloy after isothermal oxidation for 24 hours at 800 °C in 200 torr pure oxygen, with a 3 μm scale bar at the bottom right for size reference. Abundant discrete, agglomerated Mn-rich oxide protrusions with varying sizes from submicron to nearly 2 μm are observed distributing on the underlying alumina scale surface. As stated in the paper, such protrusions are rare on Fe-Mn-Al alloys with low Mn content, but become abundant when Mn concentration is high. This figure confirms that high Mn concentration in the alloy facilitates the growth of Mn-rich oxide nodules through the preformed alumina scale, supporting the conclusion that excess Mn degrades the protective performance of alumina scales on Fe-Mn-Al alloys at high temperature.
